prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, you see, sweetheart, a CPU is like the brain of a computer. It's the part that does all the thinking and decides what to do next, just like how your brain tells your body what to do. It works really hard to make sure everything runs smoothly and quickly. Even though I don’t quite understand all the fancy parts, I know it’s very important for making computers work!
